results.email: Hi, I was charged $49.99 twice this month for my Pro subscription. Can you refund the duplicate charge? Order confirmation numbers are #4471 and #4472, both dated the 3rd.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports a duplicate subscription charge and requests a refund.
results.actual_summary: The customer was charged twice ($49.99 each) for their Pro subscription on the 3rd and is requesting a refund for the duplicate charge (orders #4471 and #4472).
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue (duplicate subscription charge and refund request) as the reference, and additionally includes accurate supporting details from the original email without introducing any incorrect information.

results.email: why does my invoice say net 30 but the portal shows overdue after 15 days??? this is the second time this has happened
results.expected_summary: Customer flags a discrepancy between stated and enforced invoice payment terms.
results.actual_summary: The customer is disputing a discrepancy where their invoice states Net 30 payment terms but the portal incorrectly marks it as overdue after only 15 days, and this has happened twice.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue as the reference — a discrepancy between stated and enforced invoice payment terms — and additionally includes accurate supporting details (Net 30 vs. 15 days, recurring issue) that are consistent with the original email.

results.email: Every time I try to export my report as a PDF the app just freezes on 'Generating...' and I have to force-quit. Happens on both Chrome and Safari, macOS 14.5.
results.expected_summary: Customer's PDF export feature freezes the app across multiple browsers.
results.actual_summary: The customer is unable to export reports as PDF because the app freezes on "Generating..." across both Chrome and Safari on macOS 14.5, requiring a force-quit each time.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue (PDF export freezing the app across multiple browsers) and includes additional accurate details from the original email without contradicting anything in the reference.

results.expected_summary: Customer reports the app is not working, with no further detail provided.
results.actual_summary: The customer is reporting that the app is broken and not working.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary conveys the same core information as the reference — the customer reports the app is not working — with no missing or incorrect details.

results.email: I reset my password twice using the email link but it still says incorrect password when I try to log in. Locked out for two days now.
results.expected_summary: Customer remains locked out of their account despite resetting their password twice.
results.actual_summary: The customer has been locked out of their account for two days and is unable to log in despite resetting their password twice via email link.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference — locked out of account and password reset twice — and adds minor details (two days, via email link) that are present in the original email and don't contradict the reference.

results.email: can u chnage the email on my acount from old@gmial.com to new@gmail.com thanksss
results.expected_summary: Customer requests to change the email address associated with their account.
results.actual_summary: The customer wants to update their account email address from old@gmial.com to new@gmail.com.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core request as the reference (changing the account email address) and additionally includes the specific email addresses, which is consistent with and supported by the original email without contradicting the reference.

results.email: Love the new dashboard redesign, way easier to find what I need now. Any plans to add dark mode?
results.expected_summary: Customer gives positive feedback on the dashboard redesign and asks about dark mode.
results.actual_summary: The customer is praising the new dashboard redesign and asking if dark mode is planned as a future feature.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures both core elements — positive feedback on the dashboard redesign and the inquiry about dark mode — with no missing or incorrect information, just slightly different phrasing.

results.email: Oh great, ANOTHER update that changes where the export button is. Really appreciate having to relearn your UI every month. Fantastic work as always.
results.expected_summary: Customer sarcastically criticizes the frequency of UI changes.
results.actual_summary: The customer is expressing frustration about frequent UI changes, specifically the repeated relocation of the export button.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core issue as the reference (frustration about frequent UI changes), and the additional detail about the export button is accurate and drawn directly from the email without contradicting anything in the reference.

results.email: Hola, mi factura de este mes es incorrecta, me cobraron por 3 usuarios pero solo tenemos 2 licencias activas.
results.expected_summary: Customer says this month's invoice incorrectly charged for 3 users instead of 2.
results.actual_summary: The customer was charged for 3 users on their current invoice but states they only have 2 active licenses, and is requesting a correction.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference: incorrect charge for 3 users instead of 2 active licenses, with the added detail of "requesting a correction" which is implied in the reference and doesn't constitute a meaningful difference.

results.email: API returning 500 errors intermittently on POST /v2/orders since ~14:00 UTC today. About 1 in 5 requests. Retrying doesn't help. Need urgent help, this is affecting our checkout flow in production.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports intermittent 500 errors on a production API endpoint affecting checkout.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing intermittent 500 errors on POST /v2/orders since 14:00 UTC, affecting approximately 1 in 5 requests and disrupting their production checkout flow.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the core information from the reference (intermittent 500 errors, production API endpoint, affecting checkout) and adds specific details from the original email without contradicting anything in the reference.

results.email: This is the third email I'm sending. I need my account deleted and all my data removed per GDPR. Please confirm within 30 days as required by law.
results.expected_summary: Customer requests account deletion and data removal under GDPR, noting repeated prior contact.
results.actual_summary: The customer is requesting permanent account deletion and full data removal under GDPR regulations, and wants confirmation within the legally required 30-day window.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate captures the core GDPR account deletion and data removal request accurately, but omits the detail about repeated prior contact (third email), which is a minor but notable secondary detail in the reference.

results.email: Do you offer volume discounts for teams over 200 seats? Would like to talk to sales.
results.expected_summary: Prospective customer asks about volume discounts and requests to speak with sales.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about volume discounts for teams over 200 seats and would like to be connected with the sales team.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference: the customer is asking about volume discounts and wants to speak with sales, with the additional detail about "200 seats" being consistent with the original email and not contradicting the reference.

results.email: not sure if this is a bug or if im just dumb but the search bar doesnt find anything even when i type the exact title of a document i KNOW exists
results.expected_summary: Customer reports that search fails to find documents even with exact title matches.
results.actual_summary: The customer is reporting that the search bar returns no results even when searching for the exact title of a document that exists.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures exactly the same information as the reference: the search bar/function fails to return results even when searching for an exact document title that the customer knows exists.

results.email: downgrade me to the free plan effective immediately, and I want a prorated refund for the unused days on my current plan
results.expected_summary: Customer requests an immediate downgrade to the free plan and a prorated refund.
results.actual_summary: The customer wants to immediately downgrade their subscription to the free plan and receive a prorated refund for the remaining unused days on their current paid plan.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference — immediate downgrade to the free plan and a prorated refund — with slightly more detail about "remaining unused days" which is consistent with the original email and doesn't introduce any inaccuracies.

results.expected_summary: Customer's message consists only of '2fa', with no further context provided.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about or experiencing an issue with two-factor authentication (2FA) on their account.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary interprets "2fa" as an inquiry or issue about two-factor authentication, but the reference summary correctly notes that the message consists only of "2fa" with no further context — the candidate adds assumptions about intent that are not supported by the original message.

results.email: Hey! Are you guys hiring? I'm a huge fan of the product and would love to work there.
results.expected_summary: Customer expresses interest in job opportunities at the company.
results.actual_summary: The customer is inquiring about job opportunities at the company, expressing interest in working there as a fan of the product.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures the same core information as the reference—interest in job opportunities—and also includes the additional detail about being a fan of the product, which is present in the original email and doesn't contradict the reference.

results.email: getting a weird error, screenshot attached: 'Error 0x8007045D: cannot sync'. tried restarting, tried reinstalling, tried a different network. nothing works. this started right after your update yesterday.
results.expected_summary: Customer reports a persistent sync error that began after a recent update, despite multiple troubleshooting attempts.
results.actual_summary: Customer is experiencing a persistent 'Error 0x8007045D: cannot sync' issue that started after a recent product update, and has already attempted restarting, reinstalling, and switching networks without success.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures all the same core information as the reference — a persistent sync error triggered by a recent update, with multiple failed troubleshooting attempts — and additionally includes specific details (the error code and the individual steps tried) that are consistent with and supported by the original email, without contradicting anything in the reference.

results.email: I cancelled my subscription last week but I can still see I'm being billed. Also separately, is there a way to get an itemized receipt for tax purposes?
results.expected_summary: Customer reports being billed after cancellation and asks about obtaining an itemized receipt.
results.actual_summary: The customer is still being charged after cancelling their subscription and is also requesting an itemized receipt for tax purposes.
results.summary_score_reasoning: The candidate summary captures both core issues: continued billing after cancellation and the request for an itemized receipt, with the tax purposes detail being additional context that doesn't change the meaning.
